narrative.headline: 2022 Nissan Frontier: collision-avoidance and electrical gripes lead
narrative.summary: Owners filed 108 complaints with NHTSA. Forward collision avoidance is the most reported area at 29%, just ahead of the electrical system (26%) and power train (22%). No deaths are reported. The brakes draw fewer complaints but the most crashes (5) and injuries (2) of any area, so test them carefully.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 31 reports (29%), tied to 2 crashes and no injuries. Check the driver-assist and camera systems behave normally on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 28 reports (26%), including 1 fire, 1 crash, and 1 injury. The fire report makes this worth a careful look.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 24 reports (22%), linked to 4 crashes and no injuries. Watch for transmission trouble during the test drive.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Only 18 reports (17%) but the most serious by outcome: 5 crashes and 2 injuries, more than any other area. Test the brakes thoroughly.

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: 13 reports (12%), tied to 2 crashes and 1 injury. A smaller but recurring complaint area.
